message:
fix(crypto): migrate html_export PBKDF2 to ring + refine HKDF info slice lifetime

src/html_export/encryption.rs
- Replace pbkdf2 + sha2 crate usage with ring::pbkdf2::derive in
  both the encrypt_content path and the encryption_roundtrip test.
- Iterations flow through NonZeroU32::new(..) with an
  EncryptionError::KeyDerivation on zero (ring requires non-zero
  iterations at compile-type-level).
- PBKDF2_HMAC_SHA256 algorithm constant stands in for the
  sha2::Sha256 type parameter; key length is still 32 bytes.

src/encryption.rs
- Bind `info_components = [info]` to a local variable before
  passing &info_components to prk.expand(...). The previous
  &[info] literal dropped off the end of the expression, which the
  borrow checker flagged as a temporary with a lifetime too short
  for ring's expand() signature in downstream workspace builds.
  Same behavior, just keeps the slice alive across the call.

Cargo.lock
- Routine resolver shuffle from dropping the pbkdf2 + sha2 crate
  dependencies in html_export (minor version bumps on hashbrown
  0.36→0.37, hickory-proto 0.38→0.39, rustls 0.23.38→0.23.39,
  tinyvec_macros and similar transitive deps). No direct dependency
  changes beyond the ring migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
